title: C++ Operators
content:
### Operators in C++

Operators are symbols that perform operations on operands.

---

### Arithmetic Operators

| Operator | Name | Example | Result |
|----------|------|---------|--------|
| `+` | Addition | `5 + 2` | 7 |
| `-` | Subtraction | `5 - 2` | 3 |
| `*` | Multiplication | `5 * 2` | 10 |
| `/` | Division | `5 / 2` | 2 (integer) |
| `%` | Modulus | `5 % 2` | 1 |
| `++` | Increment | `++x` or `x++` | x + 1 |
| `--` | Decrement | `--x` or `x--` | x - 1 |

---

### Assignment Operators

```cpp
int x = 10;
x += 5;  // x = 15
x -= 3;  // x = 12
x *= 2;  // x = 24
x /= 4;  // x = 6
x %= 5;  // x = 1
```

---

### Comparison Operators

| Operator | Name | Example |
|----------|------|---------|
| `==` | Equal to | `5 == 5` is true |
| `!=` | Not equal | `5 != 3` is true |
| `>` | Greater than | `5 > 3` is true |
| `<` | Less than | `5 < 3` is false |
| `>=` | Greater or equal | `5 >= 5` is true |
| `<=` | Less or equal | `5 <= 3` is false |

---

### Logical Operators

| Operator | Name | Example | Result |
|----------|------|---------|--------|
| `&&` | AND | `true && false` | false |
| `||` | OR | `true || false` | true |
| `!` | NOT | `!true` | false |

---

### Bitwise Operators

```cpp
int a = 5 & 3;   // AND: 1
int b = 5 | 3;   // OR: 7
int c = 5 ^ 3;   // XOR: 6
int d = ~5;      // NOT: -6
int e = 5 << 1;  // Left shift: 10
int f = 5 >> 1;  // Right shift: 2
```

---

> **Key Point:** Operator precedence matters. Use parentheses `()` to make expression evaluation explicit and avoid bugs.
